claimText: India achieved its NDC target of 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuels five years ahead of the 2030 deadline.
evidenceExcerpt: Achieving 50% of cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030 (achieved five years ahead)
examUse: Statement-based question on India's NDC achievements and timelines.
distractorHooks: Claiming the target is yet to be achieved
Stating the target was 40% instead of 50%
Stating the target was achieved exactly in 2030

claimText: Solar power holds the largest share of India's non-fossil fuel installed capacity, followed by Wind and Hydro power.
evidenceExcerpt: Solar holds the largest share (39.4%) of India's non-fossil fuel installed capacity, followed by Wind (10.6%) and Hydro (10%).
examUse: Descending order arrangement question of renewable energy sources by installed capacity.
distractorHooks: Claiming Wind or Hydro has the largest share
Swapping the order of Wind and Hydro

claimText: India ranks 4th globally in overall Renewable Energy Installed Capacity and Wind Power capacity, and 3rd in Solar Power capacity.
evidenceExcerpt: India ranks 4th globally in Renewable Energy Installed Capacity and Wind Power, and 3rd in Solar Power capacity.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's global rankings in various renewable energy sectors.
distractorHooks: Swapping the rankings (e.g., 3rd in Wind, 4th in Solar)
Claiming India ranks 1st or 2nd globally in RE capacity

claimText: Rajasthan is the top renewable energy state in India, followed by Gujarat and Tamil Nadu.
evidenceExcerpt: The top 5 renewable energy states in India are Rajasthan, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Maharashtra.
examUse: Arrangement question for Indian states by renewable energy capacity.
distractorHooks: Placing Gujarat or Tamil Nadu at the top
Including non-top states like Uttar Pradesh or Madhya Pradesh in the top 3

claimText: India's Panchamrita targets for 2030 include reaching 500 GW of non-fossil energy capacity and meeting 50% of energy requirements from renewable energy.
evidenceExcerpt: Panchamrita targets for 2030 include meeting 50% of energy requirements from RE and reaching 500 GW of non-fossil energy capacity.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific goals outlined in India's Panchamrita strategy.
distractorHooks: Changing the capacity target to 450 GW or 1000 GW
Changing the target year to 2047 or 2050
Stating the target is 100% energy requirements from RE by 2030
